data.title: Stock futures drop as investors await Trump's Fed Chair pick. $81.6 million bet on nomination. SanDisk up 20.53%, Apple down 0.34%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures dropped following a mixed Thursday close as speculation heightened over President Trump's choice for Federal Reserve Chair. Betting platform Kalshi reported over $81.6 million bet on the nomination, with Warsh leading at 92% probability. Treasury bond yields for the 10-year and two-year were at 4.26% and 3.56% respectively. Markets project an 84.7% chance of the Fed keeping interest rates static in March. Index performance slumped with Dow Jones -0.84%, S&P 500 -0.92%, Nasdaq 100 -1.13%, and Russell 2000 -1.51%. Pre-market, SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust slid 0.82% to $688.32, and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF dropped 1.05% to $622.80. Apple Inc. saw a dip despite strong Q1 fiscal results, while SanDisk Corp surged by 20.53% post upbeat Q2 earnings and a joint venture extension with Kioxia Corp till Dec 31, 2034.

data.title: NEAR aims to break $1.49 resistance, with short-term target $1.41-$1.49 and medium-term forecast $1.22-$1.86.
data.description: The recent price prediction for NEAR suggests a short-term target of $1.41-$1.49 within the next week. Looking further ahead, the medium-term forecast for the next month ranges between $1.22 and $1.86. The bullish breakout level is identified at $1.49, indicating a potential increase in value. It is crucial to note the critical support level to monitor for potential price shifts.

In a recent price prediction summary, it was suggested that TRX may experience a significant increase in value in the near future.

data.title: Rio Tinto buys 68.596% stake in CBA for $902.6 million, shares valued at 10.50 South African Rand each.
data.description: Rio Tinto Plc (NYSE: RIO) and Aluminum Corporation of China Limited (Chalco) have entered an agreement with Votorantim to collectively purchase Votorantim's 68.596% controlling interest in Companhia Brasileira de Alumínio (CBA). The deal values Votorantim's stake at approximately $902.6 million, with Rio Tinto's portion estimated at around $297.8 million, pending final adjustments, regulatory authorizations, and standard terms. The acquisition will be executed through a joint venture divided 33% to Rio Tinto and 67% to Chalco. Each CBA share is priced at 10.50 South African Rand, showcasing a premium of roughly 21.2% compared to the 20-day average price of 8.67 South African Rand before January 28, 2026. Upon finalization, the joint venture will... (The complete article is accessible at Benzinga.com)

data.title: Bybit's new neobank launch with Tether-backed Pave Bank showcases crypto exchanges' move into banking sector amid regulatory challenges.
data.description: The impending launch of Bybit's neobank through Tether-supported Pave Bank underscores the significant obstacles and difficulties crypto exchanges encounter when venturing into conventional finance. This development showcases the importance of navigating regulatory requirements, with Bybit striving to enter the traditional financial realm amidst mounting challenges.

data.title: Integrow Inc. acquires Cloud Consulting Inc., enhancing their cloud solutions with a focus on consulting and professional services.
data.description: Integrow Inc. recently announced an agreement that will enhance their capabilities in cloud consulting, professional services, and customer transformation. The deal, finalized on January 30, 2026, will significantly bolster Integrow's offerings in these areas. As a prominent agentic platform specializing in integrated enterprise resource planning (ERP) and customer relationship management (CRM), Integrow is set to leverage this partnership to expand its operations and deliver a broader range of services to its clients.
